title: Shelter job
description: One assignable job at the base (FR-SHL-03/04 · GDD Part XI). Static, content-as-data definition of work a resident companion does at a room. The engine (sim/jobs.ts) is a generic interpreter — no per-job branching: each cycle it debits `consumes` from the shared stash (skipping the cycle if it is short — a job never drives the stash negative), then either credits `produces` to the stash or, for a `holdsPower` job, raises world.powerGrid. A job runs only where its `room` is built (the FR-SHL-04 capability gate) and only while its assigned worker is a companion present at the shelter. Mechanical dials (feed thresholds, power gain, spoilage rates) live in the engine; this file supplies the job. The whole system is inert without a job pool, so every prior run is byte-identical.

itemId.description: An item content id, e.g. "item.food-fresh" or "item.scrap".

qty.description: Units of the item moved per cycle — a whole, positive count.

$schema.description: Optional editor hint pointing at this schema.

id.description: Stable content id of the form job.<slug>, e.g. "job.garden".

label.description: The assignment row name shown to the player ("Tend the garden").

worldEffect.description: What the job does IN THE WORLD, in plain words — "Rooftop beds give up a little fresh food", not "+1 food/cycle". Surfaced as prose; never a number (FR-UI-02).

room.description: The room that unlocks this job (FR-SHL-04) — the job is offered and worked only where this room is built (e.g. "room.garden"). Built by a shelter-category recipe (installsRoom).

consumes.description: Drawn from the shared stash per cycle (a kitchen consumes fresh food; the generator, fuel). Omit for a pure producer. A cycle is skipped when the stash is short — a job never drives it negative.

produces.description: Banked into the shared stash per cycle (the garden's fresh food, the workshop's scrap). Omit for a hold-power job.

holdsPower.description: The generator: burn `consumes` to raise world.powerGrid instead of banking a `produces` item — the power loop's base sink, and it keeps the fridge cold.

upkeepsBarricades.description: The watch: a resident on the watchtower keeps the shelter's barricades up each cycle (the upkeep job shape), holding the wall against the T38 decay instead of banking a `produces` item.

hoursPerCycle.description: Hours of work one cycle takes — the real price and the first-pass balance dial. Defaults to 6 (DEFAULT_HOURS_PER_CYCLE). A tick completes trunc(hours / hoursPerCycle) cycles.

description.description: What the job is and why it exists (author/design reference).

notes.description: Author commentary (JSON has no comments, ADR-0002).
